top_issues.issue: Primitive Obsession — every API call passes, mutates, and threads a plain untyped opts object through the entire call chain. Retry-state fields (_retryCount, _refreshResult) are injected into this same bag at runtime, adding Temporary Field smell.

Indecent Exposure — underscore-prefixed 'private' Record methods (_getFullPayload, _getChangedPayload, _reset) are called directly from api.js and http.js. Tests directly read and mutate _fields, _changed, _previous, making Record's internal representation impossible to change without updating multiple files.

Duplicated Code — the ternary 'opts.sobject ? opts.sobject.X : opts.X' pattern for resolving IDs and types appears five times with three inconsistent resolution methods (sobject.getId(), sobject.getId(), util.findId(sobject)).

Duplicated Code — sandbox/production endpoint selection ternary or if/else appears in four separate places: authenticate(), refreshToken(), revokeToken(), and getAuthUri(). A new environment type requires changes in all four locations.

Magic String — the SAML assertion type URN 'urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:profiles:SSO:browser' is duplicated in authenticate() (line 130) and refreshToken() (line 182). A typo in either location would cause silent authentication failure.

Flag Argument — the boolean executeOnRefresh is threaded through opts to control whether _resolveWithRefresh fires the onRefresh callback. Callers cannot determine from the call site whether the callback will be invoked.
Side Effects — authenticate() and refreshToken() silently mutate the caller's OAuth object via Object.assign(opts.oauth, res). In multi-user mode this can corrupt shared OAuth references.

Lazy Element — eight trivial one-line getter/setter functions (getOAuth, setOAuth, getUsername, setUsername, getPassword, setPassword, getSecurityToken, setSecurityToken) add 30 lines of boilerplate with no transformation, validation, or encapsulation.

Magic Path Strings — the prefix '/sobjects/' is repeated 8 times in string concatenation. lib/api.js:75 also uses loginUri.replace('/oauth2/token','') to derive the Salesforce base URL — a fragile assumption about stored URI structure.
Inconsistent Style — 21 occurrences of 'let opts' vs 5 occurrences of 'const opts' in api.js, even when opts is never reassigned. Prevents linter enforcement of accidental reassignment.
What Comment — seven inline comments in getApiRequestOptions() describe what the next line does (e.g., '// set accept headers', '// process qs') rather than explaining any non-obvious business rule.

Speculative Generality — _urlRequest(data, method) is a private helper that exists solely to back four one-line public methods (getUrl, putUrl, postUrl, deleteUrl). The indirection is borderline given the current simplicity of the private function body.
Dead Code (Deprecated Method) — stream() is marked @deprecated but emits no runtime warning and specifies no concrete removal version, making it unlikely to ever be removed.

Required Setup Code — new Record() always marks all constructor-supplied fields as changed. Every caller that constructs a Record from an API response must explicitly call _reset() afterward. Missing this call causes false positives in hasChanged(). No factory method guards this invariant.
